heading: National technical assistance center; coordinating center
label: 1140q National technical assistance center; coordinating center
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior was classified to , prior to repeal by .section 777 of Pub. L. 89–329section 1132h–6 of this titlePub. L. 102–325

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From amounts appropriated under , the Secretary shall award a grant to, or enter into a contract or cooperative agreement with, an eligible entity to provide for the establishment and support of a National Center for Information and Technical Support for Postsecondary Students with Disabilities (in this subsection referred to as the “National Center”). The National Center shall carry out the duties set forth in paragraph (4).section 1140r of this title

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 2 Administration
children.text: The program under this section shall be administered by the office in the Department that administers other postsecondary education programs.

children.label: A
children.text: supporting students with disabilities in postsecondary education;

children.label: B
children.text: technical knowledge necessary for the dissemination of information in accessible formats;

children.label: C
children.text: working with diverse types of institutions of higher education, including community colleges; and

children.label: D
children.text: the subjects supported by the grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements authorized in subparts 1, 2, and 3.

children.heading: Assistance to students and families
children.label: A Assistance to students and families
i
children.text: information to assist individuals with disabilities who are prospective students of an institution of higher education in planning for postsecondary education while the students are in secondary school;

children.label: ii
children.text: information and technical assistance provided to individualized education program teams (as defined in ) for secondary school students with disabilities, and to early outreach and student services programs, including programs authorized under subparts 2, 4, and 5 of part A of subchapter IV, to support students across a broad spectrum of disabilities with the successful transition to postsecondary education;section 1414(d)(1) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: research-based supports, services, and accommodations which are available in postsecondary settings, including services provided by other agencies such as vocational rehabilitation;

children.label: iv
children.text: information on student mentoring and networking opportunities for students with disabilities; and

children.label: v
children.text: effective recruitment and transition programs at postsecondary educational institutions.

children.heading: Assistance to institutions of higher education
children.label: B Assistance to institutions of higher education
i
children.text: collection and dissemination of best and promising practices and materials for accommodating and supporting students with disabilities, including practices and materials supported by the grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements authorized under subparts 1, 2, and 3;

children.label: ii
children.text: development and provision of training modules for higher education faculty on exemplary practices for accommodating and supporting postsecondary students with disabilities across a range of academic fields, which may include universal design for learning and practices supported by the grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements authorized under subparts 1, 2, and 3; and

children.label: iii
children.text: development of technology-based tutorials for higher education faculty and staff, including new faculty and graduate students, on best and promising practices related to support and retention of students with disabilities in postsecondary education.

children.label: i
children.text: disability documentation requirements;

children.label: ii
children.text: support services available;

children.label: iii
children.text: links to financial aid;

children.label: iv
children.text: accommodations policies;

children.label: v
children.text: accessible instructional materials;

children.label: vi
children.text: other topics relevant to students with disabilities; and

children.label: vii
children.text: the information in the report described in subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Disability support services
children.label: D Disability support services
children.text: The National Center shall work with organizations and individuals with proven expertise related to disability support services for postsecondary students with disabilities to evaluate, improve, and disseminate information related to the delivery of high quality disability support services at institutions of higher education.

children.label: i
children.text: a review of the activities and the effectiveness of the programs authorized under this part;

children.label: ii
children.text: annual enrollment and graduation rates of students with disabilities in institutions of higher education from publicly reported data;

children.label: iii
children.text: recommendations for effective postsecondary supports and services for students with disabilities, and how such supports and services may be widely implemented at institutions of higher education;

children.label: iv
children.text: recommendations on reducing barriers to full participation for students with disabilities in higher education; and

children.label: v
children.text: a description of strategies with a demonstrated record of effectiveness in improving the success of such students in postsecondary education.

children.heading: Staffing of the Center
children.label: F Staffing of the Center
children.text: In hiring employees of the National Center, the National Center shall consider the expertise and experience of prospective employees in providing training and technical assistance to practitioners.

children.label: A
children.text: higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: the education of students with intellectual disabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: the development of comprehensive transition and postsecondary programs for students with intellectual disabilities; and

children.label: D
children.text: evaluation and technical assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: recommendations related to the development of standards for such programs;

children.label: B
children.text: technical assistance for such programs; and

children.label: C
children.text: evaluations for such programs.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: The program under this subsection shall be administered by the office in the Department that administers other postsecondary education programs.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 4 Duration
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into a cooperative agreement under this subsection for a period of five years.

children.label: A
children.text: serve as the technical assistance entity for all comprehensive transition and postsecondary programs for students with intellectual disabilities;

children.label: B
children.text: provide technical assistance regarding the development, evaluation, and continuous improvement of such programs;

children.label: C
children.text: develop an evaluation protocol for such programs that includes qualitative and quantitative methodologies for measuring student outcomes and program strengths in the areas of academic enrichment, socialization, independent living, and competitive or supported employment;

children.label: D
children.text: assist recipients of grants under subpart 2 in efforts to award a meaningful credential to students with intellectual disabilities upon the completion of such programs, which credential shall take into consideration unique State factors;

children.label: i
children.text: academic, vocational, social, and independent living skills;

children.label: ii
children.text: evaluation of student progress;

children.label: iii
children.text: program administration and evaluation;

children.label: iv
children.text: student eligibility; and

children.label: v
children.text: issues regarding the equivalency of a student’s participation in such programs to semester, trimester, quarter, credit, or clock hours at an institution of higher education, as the case may be;

children.label: F
children.text: analyze possible funding streams for such programs and provide recommendations regarding the funding streams;

children.label: G
children.text: develop model memoranda of agreement for use between or among institutions of higher education and State and local agencies providing funding for such programs;

children.label: H
children.text: develop mechanisms for regular communication, outreach and dissemination of information about comprehensive transition and postsecondary programs for students with intellectual disabilities under subpart 2 between or among such programs and to families and prospective students;

children.label: I
children.text: host a meeting of all recipients of grants under subpart 2 not less often than once each year; and

children.label: i
children.text: an expert in higher education;

children.label: ii
children.text: an expert in special education;

children.label: iii
children.text: a disability organization that represents students with intellectual disabilities;

children.label: iv
children.text: a representative from the National Advisory Committee on Institutional Quality and Integrity; and

children.label: v
children.text: a representative of a regional or national accreditation agency or association.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 6 Report
children.text: Not later than five years after the date of the establishment of the coordinating center under this subsection, the coordinating center shall report to the Secretary, the authorizing committees, and the National Advisory Committee on Institutional Quality and Integrity on the recommendations of the workgroup described in paragraph (5)(J).
